lens_results.1_test_to_impl_fidelity: PASS — 114 tests cover imports, runtime_checkable markers, no-ABC invariant, 4 conformance suites (per-protocol positive+negative), DispatchOptions full field inventory + type locks + frozen immutability, VaultEntry entry_id format + provenance fields + frozen immutability, module-level forbidden-import scans, and TYPE_CHECKING runtime inertness. Sage mid-Warrior amendment from `isinstance(proto, abc.ABCMeta)` → `type(proto) is not abc.ABCMeta` correctly applied (line 188): the original form was unreachable because typing._ProtocolMeta inherits ABCMeta in CPython 3.12. Identity-check form catches plain abc.ABC regression while admitting _ProtocolMeta. No tests pass by accident observed.
lens_results.2_sdk_integration: PASS — zero Claude Agent SDK imports in src/bonfire/protocols.py or tests/unit/test_protocols.py. AgentBackend contract documents the SDK-backed default in docstring prose only; no binding leaks into the protocol surface.
lens_results.3_identity_seal_immutability: PASS — DispatchOptions (line 54) and VaultEntry (line 78) both declare `model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)`. TestDispatchOptions and TestVaultEntry both include ValidationError-on-mutation tests. Frozen invariant byte-for-byte matches private V1.
lens_results.4_three_adaptations: PASS — git grep confirms zero `BON-[0-9]+`, zero `bonfire-beta`, zero `ProjectWorkflowConfig`, zero `constraint-index.md` in protocols.py. 4 `@runtime_checkable` decorators. Only TYPE_CHECKING-guarded imports reference bonfire.models. Adaptations from V1: (a) C47/C48/C21 constraint labels stripped from module docstring (L13-15); (b) `(BON-126B)` and `(BON-138)` inline comments removed (L60, L64); (c) `Session 022` provenance note replaced with generic text (L74-75).
lens_results.5_cross_check_v1: PASS — read /home/ishtar/Projects/bonfire/src/bonfire/protocols.py in full. Protocol method signatures, parameter names, return types, keyword-only markers, docstring bodies, field types, field defaults, Field(default_factory=...) usage, _vault_entry_id helper, ConfigDict(frozen=True) placement, __all__ entries AND ORDER (alphabetical), class declaration order — all identical. No behavioral drift.
lens_results.6_warrior_conservatism: PASS — 194 LOC matches V1 exactly. No speculative abstractions, no helper classes, no factory registries, no __init_subclass__ machinery. Pure protocol surface + two frozen value types + one private helper. Warrior correctly caught the Knight-A metaclass defect during GREEN and escalated to Sage for amendment rather than working around it in impl — textbook test-first discipline.
lens_results.7_pr_metadata_hygiene: PASS — PR base is `v0.1` (per meta.json baseRefName). Commit `4516ef8` title `bon-332: W2.2 protocols transfer — AgentBackend + VaultBackend + QualityGate + StageHandler` cites ticket. Sage decisions doc `docs/audit/sage-decisions/bon-332-sage-20260418T004817Z.md` (227 LOC) present in diff with full adjudication trail. Three files changed, +1290 LOC, no deletions. Clean atomic transfer.

findings.summary: ALL_PROTOCOLS list built at import time; if a 5th protocol ships without updating this list, conformance/runtime-checkable sweeps silently skip it.
findings.detail: Non-blocking. ALL_PROTOCOLS = [AgentBackend, VaultBackend, QualityGate, StageHandler] is constructed at module load. A future 5th protocol would need explicit addition here to participate in the parametrized sweeps (runtime_checkable marker, no-ABC metaclass, etc.). TestModuleConstraints does assert specific names exist in source text, which partially mitigates drift. Acceptable for current scope; note for future transfers.
findings.suggestion: Consider in a follow-up: derive ALL_PROTOCOLS from bonfire.protocols.__all__ minus the two known value types, and assert len == 4. Or add a test that counts @runtime_checkable decorators in source and asserts it matches len(ALL_PROTOCOLS). Do NOT block merge for this.

rationale: Seven-lens review clean. 376/376 tests green, ruff clean, scrub clean, 4 @runtime_checkable decorators, 4 protocols, 2 frozen supporting types, __all__ alphabetical, zero private refs, zero forbidden imports, zero SDK bindings. Public mirrors private V1 byte-for-byte modulo the three documented adaptations. Sage's mid-Warrior metaclass amendment was correctly motivated and correctly applied — exactly the kind of adversarial catch the dual-Knight + Sage synthesis is designed to produce. Warrior kept impl minimal (194 LOC, V1 verbatim). Sage decisions doc gives a durable audit trail. Only one INFO-severity observation (W-001), non-blocking. Ready for squash-merge into v0.1 once code-reviewer lens also approves.
